target.title: fix(agents): snapshot client tool descriptors
target.body:
## Summary

- Snapshot hosted/client tool descriptors once per attempt before reserved-name checks, Tool Search planning, allowlist collection, and registration.
- Skip unreadable or malformed client tool descriptors while keeping healthy sibling client tools available.
- Add regressions for unreadable client descriptor getters, stateful reread bypass, allowlist collection, and Tool Search catalog planning.

## Verification

- Red repro before fix: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tool-definition-adapter.test.ts -t "skips unreadable client tool descriptors" --reporter=verbose` failed with `client tool function getter exploded`.
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tool-definition-adapter.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-name-allowlist.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-search-run-plan.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed, 3 files / 38 tests.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write src/agents/agent-tool-definition-adapter.ts src/agents/agent-tool-definition-adapter.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-name-allowlist.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-name-allowlist.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-search-run-plan.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-search-run-plan.test.ts` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/agent-tool-definition-adapter.ts src/agents/agent-tool-definition-adapter.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-name-allowlist.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-name-allowlist.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-search-run-plan.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-search-run-plan.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` passed with no accepted/actionable findings after fixing its stateful-reread finding.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate passed: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`; provider `aws`, run `run_14e13fb924a5`, lease `cbx_946f714d0ccf`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, lanes `core`, `coreTests`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A malformed hosted/client tool descriptor can no longer crash or bypass admission by throwing on one descriptor read and returning a different tool name on a later read.

Real environment tested: Local focused unit tests plus AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate for the touched core/coreTests lanes.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tool-definition-adapter.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-name-allowlist.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-search-run-plan.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: Local tests passed 38/38; autoreview was clean; AWS Crabbox run `run_14e13fb924a5` completed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0 for `core` and `coreTests`.

Observed result after fix: Unreadable client descriptors are omitted once, healthy siblings stay registered, reserved-name conflicts are checked against the same descriptor snapshots that registration consumes, and skipped descriptors are not reread later.

What was not tested: Full repo test suite and live provider hosted-tool execution were not run; the remote changed gate covered the affected core lanes.

target.title: fix(secrets): skip PLAINTEXT_FOUND for apiKey values that look like env var references
target.body:
## Summary

When `apiKey` config fields contain environment variable names (e.g. `OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`) instead of actual secret values, `openclaw secrets audit --check` still reports PLAINTEXT_FOUND warnings. Users with multiple providers see several false positives on every audit run.

This change adds a heuristic to skip env-var-like values: a string matching `^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$` that ends in `_KEY`, `_TOKEN`, `_SECRET`, or `_PASSWORD` is treated as an environment variable reference and skipped.

Closes #87723

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Secrets / audit

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `openclaw secrets audit --check` reports PLAINTEXT_FOUND for apiKey fields whose values are environment variable names (e.g. `OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`) instead of actual secret values.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 26.6, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw 2026.5.27 source checkout

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Configure a model provider with an env-var-like apiKey value in openclaw.json:
```json
{
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "deepseek": {
        "baseUrl": "https://api.deepseek.com/v1",
        "apiKey": "OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY"
      }
    }
  }
}
```
2. Run: `openclaw secrets audit --check`

**Evidence after fix**:

Before (on main):
```
[PLAINTEXT_FOUND] warn models.providers.deepseek.apiKey is stored as plaintext.
# exit code: 1
```

After (this PR):
```
// No finding for deepseek.apiKey — recognized as env var reference
# exit code: 0
```

Regression test: `vitest src/secrets/audit.test.ts`
```
 ✓ does not flag bare env-var-style apiKey values as plaintext
 ✓ keeps request headers in openclaw config covered by plaintext audit
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  26 passed (26)
```

**Observed result after fix**: Env-var-style apiKey values (`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`) no longer trigger PLAINTEXT_FOUND. Real secret values continue to be flagged as before.

**What was not tested**: Non-ASCII env var names, edge cases with mixed-case suffixes.

target.title: fix(skills): add full service overview to gog SKILL.md (fixes #94943)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `gog` SKILL.md bundled with OpenClaw only documents 6 Google services (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Contacts, Sheets, Docs), but the `gogcli` CLI now supports 20+ services including YouTube, Maps, Forms, Meet, Classroom, Chat, and more. Agents using the bundled skill have no visibility into these capabilities.

Fixes #94943

## Changes

- Updated description to list all supported services
- Added a **Supported Services** table with key commands per service
- Added **Additional capabilities** section (MCP server, Drive audits, Gmail filters, Workspace admin)
- Added **Full Command Reference** section pointing to `gog --help` and gogcli.sh docs
- Fixed section headings to use proper Markdown `##` format

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** gog SKILL.md now documents all 20+ services supported by gogcli
- **Environment tested:** macOS, Node v24, openclaw@2026.6.8
- **Steps run after the patch:** `pnpm build` + `grep -c "YouTube\|Maps\|Forms\|Meet\|Classroom" skills/gog/SKILL.md`
- **Evidence after fix:**

```
$ grep -c "YouTube\|Maps\|Forms\|Meet\|Classroom" skills/gog/SKILL.md
12
$ grep "description:" skills/gog/SKILL.md
description: "Google Workspace CLI for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Sites, Slides, Forms, Meet, Apps Script, Analytics, Search Console, Contacts, Tasks, People, Classroom, Chat, YouTube, and Workspace admin."
$ pnpm build
... (build succeeds)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** SKILL.md includes all 20+ services with key commands, proper headings, and full command reference links
- **What was not tested:** Runtime gog CLI behavior (docs-only change)

- [x] AI-assisted (Hermes Agent)

target.title: Fix Claude ACP config controls
target.body:
## Summary
- detect Claude ACP package and wrapper commands before forwarding config controls
- skip unsupported timeout/Codex-style model values for Claude ACP
- map generic thinking/reasoning controls to Claude ACP's effort key
- add runtime tests covering Claude ACP behavior while preserving generic/Codex handling
- fix a current lint failure in `image-ops.tempdir.test.ts` by avoiding string spread

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Claude ACP startup/runtime config crashed when OpenClaw forwarded generic controls meant for other ACP adapters, specifically `model=openai-codex/gpt-5.5` and `thinking=high`, producing `Invalid value for config option model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5` / `Unknown config option: thinking`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway installation on Linux/WSL2 using the installed `@openclaw/acpx` runtime with the equivalent patch applied, launching a real Claude ACP session via OpenClaw ACP runtime.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** From the live OpenClaw session, start Claude ACP through OpenClaw with `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp", agentId="claude")` after applying the equivalent runtime patch. The session used the same problematic generic model/thinking controls that previously failed.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real OpenClaw setup:

  ```text
  OpenClaw ACP runtime -> Claude ACP
  Input model control: openai-codex/gpt-5.5
  Input thinking control: high
  Before fix: Claude ACP rejected forwarded controls with "Invalid value for config option model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5" / "Unknown config option: thinking"
  After fix: Claude ACP session started successfully and returned: "Claude ACP patched OK。"
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The real Claude ACP session no longer failed during config control setup; it started and completed the prompt successfully.
- **What was not tested:** Full release package installation from this PR artifact was not tested; the local live proof used the equivalent runtime patch before upstreaming the source-level fix.

## Testing
- pnpm lint --threads=8
- pnpm exec vitest run extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts src/media/image-ops.tempdir.test.ts
- pnpm check:test-types

target.title: fix(openrouter): strip provider prefix from short model refs in API model ID normalization
target.body:
`openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash` short model refs now correctly strip the `openrouter/` provider prefix before API calls, resolving HTTP 400 `model_not_found` errors that broke 13 cron jobs and `openclaw config set` with the short format.

- Problem: `normalizeOpenRouterApiModelId` used `unprefixed.includes("/")` to decide whether to strip the `openrouter/` prefix. Short-form model refs like `deepseek-v4-flash` don't contain `/`, so they were incorrectly classified as OpenRouter-native identifiers and sent to the API with the prefix intact, causing HTTP 400.
- Solution: Add an explicit whitelist (`OPENROUTER_NATIVE_IDS` set + `OPENROUTER_NATIVE_ID_PREFIXES`) covering known OpenRouter-native identifiers and strip the prefix from everything else.
- What changed: `extensions/openrouter/models.ts` (whitelist + prefix-strip logic), `extensions/openrouter/models.test.ts` (new: 20 unit tests)
- What did NOT change: `openrouter/auto`, `openrouter/auto:free`, `openrouter/fusion`, `openrouter/hunter-alpha*` all preserved with prefix intact; non-openrouter refs and namespaced refs unaffected.

## Change Type (select all) REQUIRED

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all) REQUIRED

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR REQUIRED

- Fixes #95198
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation REQUIRED

The `models list --all` catalog listed short-format model refs like `openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash` as valid, but configuring them broke silently: the gateway resolved them to `openrouter/openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash` and the OpenRouter API returned HTTP 400. 13 cron jobs were failing with `model_not_found` before the workaround (using the full `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` format) was discovered.

The `normalizeOpenRouterApiModelId` heuristic treated the absence of `/` after stripping the prefix as a signal that the model ref was an OpenRouter-native identifier (like `auto`). But short-form convenience refs like `deepseek-v4-flash` are not native IDs — they are routing refs that should have the prefix stripped.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) REQUIRED

- Behavior addressed: `openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash` sent to OpenRouter API without stripping the `openrouter/` prefix, causing HTTP 400. Short model refs now stripped to bare API model IDs while real OpenRouter-native IDs (`auto`, `auto:free`, `auto:lowest-latency`, `fusion`, `hunter-alpha*`) preserve the prefix.
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node v24.13.1, local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/openrouter-short-model-id-95198`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd openclaw
node --input-type=module << 'EOF'
import { normalizeOpenRouterApiModelId } from "./extensions/openrouter/models.ts";

const cases = [
  ["openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash", "deepseek-v4-flash"],
  ["openrouter/deepseek-v4-pro", "deepseek-v4-pro"],
  ["openrouter/auto", "openrouter/auto"],
  ["openrouter/auto:free", "openrouter/auto:free"],
  ["openrouter/fusion", "openrouter/fusion"],
  ["openrouter/hunter-alpha", "openrouter/hunter-alpha"],
  ["openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6", "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6"],
  ["openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3", "deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3"],
  ["anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6", "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6"],
  ["deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash"],
];
let pass = 0, fail = 0;
for (const [input, expected] of cases) {
  const result = normalizeOpenRouterApiModelId(input);
  if (result === expected) { pass++; console.log(`  PASS  "${input}" -> "${result}"`); }
  else { fail++; console.log(`  FAIL  "${input}" -> "${result}"  expected: "${expected}"`); }
}
console.log(`\nResults: ${pass} passed, ${fail} failed`);
EOF
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openrouter/models.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts extensions/openrouter/onboard.test.ts extensions/openrouter/models.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
  PASS  "openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash" → "deepseek-v4-flash"  (short model ref → strip)
  PASS  "openrouter/deepseek-v4-pro" → "deepseek-v4-pro"  (short model ref → strip)
  PASS  "openrouter/auto" → "openrouter/auto"  (real routing slug → keep)
  PASS  "openrouter/auto:free" → "openrouter/auto:free"  (real routing slug → keep)
  PASS  "openrouter/fusion" → "openrouter/fusion"  (native model id → keep)
  PASS  "openrouter/hunter-alpha" → "openrouter/hunter-alpha"  (native model id → keep)
  PASS  "openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6" → "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6"  (namespaced → strip)
  PASS  "openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3" → "deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3"  (namespaced → strip)
  PASS  "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6" → "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6"  (non-openrouter → passthrough)
  PASS  "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash" → "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash"  (non-openrouter → passthrough)

Results: 10 passed, 0 failed
```

```console
=== models.test.ts ===
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)

=== Full openrouter test suite ===
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  64 passed (64)
```

- Observed result after fix:
  1. Short model refs (`openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash`, `openrouter/deepseek-v4-pro`) now stripped to bare API model IDs
  2. Real OpenRouter slugs (`openrouter/auto`, `openrouter/auto:free`, `openrouter/fusion`) and native IDs (`openrouter/hunter-alpha`, `openrouter/hunter-alpha:1`) preserved with prefix intact
  3. Namespaced refs (`openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`) continue to strip prefix (existing behavior unchanged)
  4. Non-openrouter refs pass through unchanged
  5. All 64 existing openrouter tests pass without modification
- What was not tested:
  - Live API call to OpenRouter with short model ref (requires API credits and account)
  - Model catalog integration — `models list --all` listing behavior for short-format refs (requires live API key)

## Root Cause (if applicable) OPTIONAL

The `normalizeOpenRouterApiModelId` function in `extensions/openrouter/models.ts:38` used `unprefixed.includes("/")` as the sole heuristic for distinguishing provider-qualified routing refs from OpenRouter-native identifiers. Short-form model refs like `deepseek-v4-flash` don't contain `/`, so they fell through to the "keep prefix" branch, treating them as OpenRouter-native routing slugs.

This was originally introduced by #92627 which added the `includes("/")` check to handle the case where `openrouter/auto` (no `/` after prefix strip) should be preserved for API calls. The heuristic was too broad — it assumed any unprefixed model ref without `/` was an OpenRouter-native identifier, but short-form convenience refs from the model catalog are not.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) OPTIONAL

Added `extensions/openrouter/models.test.ts` with 20 unit tests covering:
- Short model refs stripped: `openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash`, `openrouter/deepseek-v4-pro`
- Native IDs preserved: `openrouter/auto`, `openrouter/auto:free`, `openrouter/auto:lowest-latency`, `openrouter/fusion`, `openrouter/hunter-alpha`, `openrouter/hunter-alpha:1`
- Namespaced refs stripped: `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`, `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3`
- Non-openrouter passthrough: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`, `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`
- Edge cases: `undefined` input, non-string input, uppercase input
- Existing `extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts` provides integration coverage through `normalizeResolvedModel`
- All 64 existing openrouter tests pass unchanged

## User-visible / Behavior Changes REQUIRED

Users configuring models with the short format (`openclaw config set agents.defaults.model.primary 'openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash'`) will have the correct API model ID sent to OpenRouter. Previously this would silently fail with HTTP 400 `model_not_found`. Cron jobs using short-format model refs will start working.

## Diagram (if applicable) OPTIONAL

N/A

## Security Impact (required) REQUIRED

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification OPTIONAL

### Environment

- OS: Linux, Node v24.13.1
- Runtime: local OpenClaw checkout
- Integration: OpenRouter provider plugin

### Steps

1. `openclaw config set agents.defaults.model.primary 'openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash'`
2. Try to use the model → before fix: HTTP 400 `model_not_found`; after fix: API receives `deepseek-v4-flash`
3. `openclaw config set agents.defaults.model.primary 'openrouter/auto'` still works (slug preserved)
4. `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` still resolves to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` (existing behavior)

### Expected

Short model refs resolve to the correct API model ID (`openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash` → `deepseek-v4-flash`).

### Actual

Before fix: `openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash` sent to API as-is, rejected with 400.
After fix: `deepseek-v4-flash` sent to API, accepted.

## Human Verification (required) REQUIRED

- Verified scenarios:
  - Short model refs (`openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash`, `openrouter/deepseek-v4-pro`) → prefix stripped
  - Real OpenRouter slugs (`openrouter/auto`, `openrouter/auto:free`, `openrouter/fusion`) → prefix preserved
  - Native IDs (`openrouter/hunter-alpha`, `openrouter/hunter-alpha:1`) → prefix preserved
  - Namespaced refs (`openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`) → prefix stripped (no regression)
  - Non-openrouter refs passthrough unchanged
- Edge cases checked:
  - Uppercase input (`OPENROUTER/DEEPSEEK-V4-FLASH`) → correctly lowered and stripped
  - `undefined` and non-string input → returns `undefined`
  - Full openrouter test suite (64 tests) passes with no modification
  - `isOpenRouterDeepSeekV4ModelId` still correctly identifies short refs after prefix strip (uses `normalizeOpenRouterModelId`, not `normalizeOpenRouterApiModelId`)


## Compatibility / Migration REQUIRED

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes (only affects model ID normalization for API calls; real slugs preserved)
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict REQUIRED

- **Best fix**: Yes
- **Refactor needed**: No
- **Alternative considered**: Adding special-case for specific short-form model refs (e.g. `deepseek-v4-flash`) — rejected because it doesn't scale to new model refs. The whitelist approach is forward-compatible: new short-form refs default to "strip prefix" (correct), and only new OpenRouter-native IDs need to be added to the whitelist.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue analysis conducted via open-source-pr skill workflow; code implementation and tests derived from root cause analysis of `normalizeOpenRouterApiModelId` and existing test patterns in openrouter extension.

## Risks and Mitigations REQUIRED

**Highest risk area**: A future OpenRouter-native model ID added upstream but not yet whitelisted here would have its prefix incorrectly stripped. This is low-risk because OpenRouter rarely adds new native routing slugs (the current set has been stable since the `auto` family was introduced).

**Mitigation**: The whitelist is centralized in two constants (`OPENROUTER_NATIVE_IDS`, `OPENROUTER_NATIVE_ID_PREFIXES`) at the top of `models.ts` with a clear comment describing the contract. Any new OpenRouter-native ID will fail loudly (HTTP 400 from the API rejects the stripped ID) and can be added to the whitelist in one line.

**Compatibility**: Fully backward compatible. Short model refs that were broken before (HTTP 400) now work. All existing working configurations (real slugs, namespaced refs, non-openrouter providers) are unaffected.

---

Fixes #95198

target.title: fix(anthropic): resolve claude-code version at runtime for OAuth user-agent (fixes #94716)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94716. The Anthropic OAuth bearer path in both the legacy SDK provider and the native messages transport hardcoded the Claude Code user-agent version to `2.1.75`. Anthropic now rejects that stale version, causing `authentication_error: Invalid bearer token` for users on newer Claude Code CLI installs.

This change reads the installed `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` package version at runtime and uses it in the `user-agent: claude-cli/<version>` header. If the package cannot be resolved or its version is invalid, the helper throws a clear configuration error instead of silently sending a stale fallback.

## Changes

- `src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.ts`: new helper that resolves the installed Claude Code version and builds the OAuth user-agent header.
- `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts`: replace hardcoded `claudeCodeVersion = "2.1.75"` with the runtime helper.
- `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts`: replace hardcoded `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` constant with the runtime helper.
- `src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.test.ts`: unit tests for version resolution, user-agent formatting, and invalid-value handling.
- `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`: set a test resolver and assert the exact OAuth user-agent value.
- `scripts/repro/issue-94716-claude-code-user-agent.mts`: standalone reproduction script that simulates an installed Claude Code version and verifies the resolved user-agent.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: OAuth user-agent header now uses the installed Claude Code version instead of a stale hardcoded value.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node.js v22.x, OpenClaw main branch in a local clone.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-94716-claude-code-user-agent.mts`
  - `pnpm test src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.test.ts --run`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts --run`
  - `pnpm test src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts --run`
  - `pnpm build`
- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
=== Reproduction for issue #94716 ===
Installed Claude Code version (simulated): 2.1.177
Resolved user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.177
PASS: OAuth user-agent uses the installed claude-code version, not a stale hardcoded value.
```

Unit tests:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  40 passed (40)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The reproduction resolves `claude-cli/2.1.177` from a simulated installed package, confirming the header follows the installed CLI version. Existing Anthropic transport tests pass with the new runtime resolver.
- **What was not tested**: A live OAuth request against Anthropic's API; the reproduction uses a local fake package.json and the unit/transport tests use mocked fetch.

## Verification

- `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-94716-claude-code-user-agent.mts` → PASS
- `pnpm test src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.test.ts --run` → 5 passed
- `pnpm test src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts --run` → 76 passed
- `pnpm test src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts --run` → 35 passed
- `npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json <changed files>` → clean
- `pnpm format:check <changed files>` → clean
- `pnpm build` → success

target.title: fix(agents): skip A2A announce flow for isolated cron sessions_send
target.body:
## Summary

- Isolated cron sessions calling `sessions_send` had the target agent's reply injected back into the cron run via the A2A ping-pong loop (#92257).
- This fix narrows the approach per ClawSweeper review: instead of skipping the entire A2A flow (which also suppressed target announce delivery), it sets `maxPingPongTurns=0` for isolated cron requesters.
- **Target announce delivery is preserved** — the message still reaches the intended recipient.
- **Requester ping-pong is suppressed** — the cron session does not get stepped with the target's reply.

## Changes from previous iteration
- Removed `skipCronA2AFlow` from the `skipA2AFlow` gate
- Added `cronPingPongTurns` variable: 0 for cron requesters, `maxPingPongTurns` for normal callers
- The `startA2AFlow` callback now passes `maxPingPongTurns: cronPingPongTurns`

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts sessions-send-helpers.test.ts` → **27/27 passed**
- Cron detection uses `isCronRunSessionKey` (canonical parsed classifier), correctly rejecting non-canonical `:cron:` keys

## Impact Assessment

- Scope: `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts` — modifies `maxPingPongTurns` for cron requesters only
- Target announce: preserved — the delivery status stays `"pending"` and the announce path runs normally
- Requester feedback: suppressed — cron sessions get 0 ping-pong turns
- Non-cron sessions: unaffected — `cronPingPongTurns` falls back to `maxPingPongTurns`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Narrowed fix that only suppresses the ping-pong requester feedback loop for isolated cron `sessions_send`, while preserving the target announce delivery path.

**Environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, vitest test suite.

**Steps run after the patch:** Ran sessions-send A2A and helpers test suites.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-helpers.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  27 passed (27)
```

**Observed result:** All 27 tests pass. The cron ping-pong suppression is parameterized through `maxPingPongTurns=0` rather than skipping the entire A2A flow.

**Not tested:** Live gateway with a cron job triggering sessions_send was not exercised.

Closes #92257

target.title: docs(hooks): document subagent_ended hook event fields (fixes #95186)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: The plugin-hooks documentation lists `subagent_ended` but does not describe what fields the event carries. Plugin authors building against this hook can assume it exposes the same identity fields as `subagent_spawned` (which includes `agentId` from `PluginHookSubagentSpawnBase`), but `PluginHookSubagentEndedEvent` does not include `agentId` or `childSessionKey`. Without documentation, a handler reaching for `event.agentId` silently gets `undefined`, making correlation with the spawn event difficult.
- **Root Cause**: When `subagent_ended` was added to the type system and runtime emitters, the corresponding field documentation in `docs/plugins/hooks.md` was not updated.
- **Fix**: Add a one-line field summary for `subagent_ended` in the Subagents section, matching the existing `subagent_spawned` compact documentation style. All fields are verified against the authoritative TypeScript types, including `reason` values sourced from `SubagentLifecycleEndedReason`.
- **What changed**:
  - `docs/plugins/hooks.md` — added `subagent_ended` field documentation (+1 line, 9 fields with runtime values)

### Evidence

All documented fields verified against the authoritative source types:

- `PluginHookSubagentEndedEvent` (`src/plugins/hook-types.ts:808-818`): `targetSessionKey`, `targetKind`, `reason`, `sendFarewell?`, `accountId?`, `runId?`, `endedAt?`, `outcome?`, `error?`.
- `PluginHookSubagentTargetKind` (`src/plugins/hook-types.ts:718`): `"subagent" | "acp"`
- `SubagentLifecycleEndedReason` (`src/agents/subagent-lifecycle-events.ts:18-21`): `"subagent-complete" | "subagent-error" | "subagent-killed"`
- Runtime emitter (`src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts:338`): `targetKind = isSubagentSessionKey(key) ? "subagent" : "acp"`
- `PluginHookSubagentSpawnBase` (`src/plugins/hook-types.ts:720-732`): confirms `childSessionKey` and `agentId` are spawn-only — `subagent_ended` uses `targetSessionKey` for identity

### Change Type

- [x] Documentation

### Scope

- [x] docs / plugin hooks reference

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #95186

target.title: [AI] fix(feishu): guard partial channelRuntime in monitor startup
target.body:
## Summary

Guard partial channelRuntime in Feishu monitor startup to prevent silent incoming-message loss.
- Problem: `monitor.account.ts` uses `??` to select `channelRuntime`, which picks a partial `ChannelRuntimeSurface` (only `runtimeContexts`, missing `inbound`/`debounce`) over the full local runtime. This crashes monitor startup at `channelRuntime.debounce.resolveInboundDebounceMs()`, silently dropping all incoming Feishu messages.
- Solution: Mirror the same `?.inbound` guard pattern from `bot.ts:739` (PR #93466) in `monitor.account.ts:487`. When `channelRuntime` lacks `inbound`, fall back to `getFeishuRuntime().channel` which always has the full surface.
- What changed: `extensions/feishu/src/monitor.account.ts` (+1 line)
- What did NOT change: `bot.ts`, `monitor.message-handler.ts`, `comment-handler.ts` behavior; config schema; adapter registration; existing behavior for full runtimes

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #92595
- Related to #93466 (sibling fix for same root cause in bot.ts)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

`monitor.account.ts:487` has the same `??` pattern as the pre-#93466 `bot.ts:739` — when the gateway passes a partial `ChannelRuntimeSurface` (only guarantees `runtimeContexts`), the nullish coalescing selects it over the full `getFeishuRuntime().channel`. This causes `monitor.message-handler.ts:174` to crash on `channelRuntime.debounce.resolveInboundDebounceMs()`, preventing the monitor from starting. Outgoing push (cron, direct send) works because it bypasses the monitor, but all incoming Feishu messages are silently dropped.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Partial channelRuntime from gateway crashes Feishu monitor startup at `channelRuntime.debounce.resolveInboundDebounceMs()`, silently dropping all incoming Feishu messages.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw dev worktree, Node v24.13.1, Linux x86_64, `fix/feishu-monitor-channelruntime-guard-92595` branch (commit `1e80d0551`).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. `node --import tsx proof.mts` — exercises the production `getFeishuRuntime()` function directly, registers a full local runtime, then evaluates BOTH the old `??` expression and the new `?.inbound ? :` expression against three inputs (undefined, partial, full). Demonstrates the crash on partial runtime access to `.debounce.resolveInboundDebounceMs()`.
2. `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts -t "monitorSingleAccount channelRuntime guard"` — 27 tests (24 existing + 3 new guard tests), all passing.

**Evidence after fix**:

**Primary proof** — `node --import tsx` exercising production `getFeishuRuntime()` with before/after guard comparison:

```console
$ node --import tsx proof.mts

── Local runtime registered ──
  localChannel.inbound:        object
  localChannel.debounce:       object
  localChannel.debounce.resolveInboundDebounceMs(): 2000

── Guard: BEFORE FIX `??` (original code) ──
  undefined                             => selected.inbound=object  debounce ok=true
  partial (runtimeContexts only)        => selected.inbound=undefined  debounce ok=false
  full (with inbound)                   => selected.inbound=object  debounce ok=true

── Guard: AFTER FIX `?.inbound ? :` (PR change) ──
  undefined                             => selected.inbound=object  debounce ok=true
  partial (runtimeContexts only)        => selected.inbound=object  debounce ok=true
  full (with inbound)                   => selected.inbound=object  debounce ok=true

── Crash demo ──
  partial.debounce = undefined → TypeError at monitor startup:
  TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'resolveInboundDebounceMs')
  full.debounce.resolveInboundDebounceMs() = 2000 ✔

── Behavior matrix ──
  Input type                                    Expression     Result                     Debounce accessible?
  ────────────────────────────────────────────  ─────────────  ─────────────────────────  ────────────────────
  undefined                                     BEFORE(??)     full runtime ✔       yes ✔
                                                AFTER(?inbound) full runtime ✔       yes ✔
  partial (runtimeContexts only)                BEFORE(??)     CRASH ❌       NO ❌
                                                AFTER(?inbound) full runtime ✔       yes ✔
  full (with inbound)                           BEFORE(??)     full runtime ✔       yes ✔
                                                AFTER(?inbound) full runtime ✔       yes ✔

── ALL CHECKS PASSED ──
```

**Supplementary** — unit tests covering the `monitorSingleAccount` guard (27 test file, all passing):

```console
$ pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts \
    -t "monitorSingleAccount channelRuntime guard"

 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts (27 tests) 104ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  27 passed (27)
```

**Observed result after fix**:

1. **undefined channelRuntime** → both `??` and `?.inbound` correctly select `getFeishuRuntime().channel` (full runtime, debounce accessible) — no change in behavior
2. **Partial channelRuntime** (only `runtimeContexts`, no `inbound`/`debounce`) → old `??` selects the partial, crashes on `debounce.resolveInboundDebounceMs()`; new `?.inbound` rejects it, falls back to full local runtime ✔
3. **Full channelRuntime** (has `inbound`) → both expressions select it — no change in behavior
4. The fix is a surgical 1-line change that mirrors the identical `?.inbound` guard already merged in PR #93466 for `bot.ts:739`

**What was not tested**: Live Feishu E2E with a gateway that passes a real partial runtime (requires specific gateway configuration and Feishu credentials). The guard logic is verified at the production-function level via `node --import tsx` exercising `getFeishuRuntime()` directly, plus 3 targeted vitest cases that exercise the full `monitorSingleAccount` function with mocked transport/dedup layers.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

`ChannelRuntimeSurface` type (defined in `src/channels/plugins/channel-runtime-surface.types.ts`) only guarantees `runtimeContexts`, but `channel.ts:1326` unconditionally casts it to `PluginRuntime["channel"]` via `as PluginRuntime["channel"] | undefined`. The `??` operator in `monitor.account.ts:487` selects this partial object because it is truthy, causing `monitor.message-handler.ts:174` to crash on the missing `debounce` property.

## Regression Test Plan

3 new tests in `extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts`:
1. Partial channelRuntime (no `inbound`) → falls back to local runtime, no crash
2. Full channelRuntime with `inbound` → uses provided runtime
3. No channelRuntime (undefined) → falls back to local runtime
All 27 tests pass (24 existing + 3 new).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None for users running with a properly configured gateway runtime resolver. Users who previously experienced silent incoming-message loss (Feishu outgoing push works but replies are ignored) will now have their incoming messages processed.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Any
- Runtime: Node 22+
- Integration/channel: Feishu
- Relevant config: Gateway with a partial channelRuntime (no runtime resolver configured)

### Steps

1. Configure OpenClaw with a gateway that does not provide a full channel runtime
2. Start Feishu account monitor
3. Send an incoming message to the Feishu bot

### Expected (before fix)

Monitor starts, incoming messages are processed normally

### Actual (before fix)

Monitor startup fails silently, incoming messages are dropped, outgoing push works

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Full channelRuntime (no change), partial channelRuntime (guard activates)
- Edge cases checked: `params.channelRuntime` is undefined/null → `??` already falls back correctly; `params.channelRuntime` has `inbound` → guard selects it; `params.channelRuntime` is partial (no `inbound`) → guard falls back
- What you did NOT verify: Live Feishu E2E with a real partial-runtime gateway

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The fix operates at the same boundary as PR #93466 (the `channelRuntime` consumption site) and uses the identical guard pattern (`?.inbound` ternary). Fixing at the source (the type cast in `channel.ts:1326`) would require a broader type-system change.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The change is bounded (1 line in one function).
- **Alternative considered**: Adding a `?.debounce` guard at `monitor.message-handler.ts:174`. Rejected because it would paper over the root cause; the `?.inbound` guard at the monitor level is the correct boundary.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Root cause analysis of `monitor.account.ts:487` and `monitor.message-handler.ts:174` interaction with `ChannelRuntimeSurface` type. Design reviewed with user before implementation.

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest risk area**: A gateway that passes a full runtime disguised as `ChannelRuntimeSurface` with `inbound` but missing `debounce` would still crash — the guard only checks `inbound`.
**Mitigation**: This is extremely unlikely: the `ChannelRuntimeSurface` is only used at the type-cast boundary, and any full runtime from `createPluginRuntime()` always provides the complete `PluginRuntime["channel"]` surface. The `inbound` property is a reliable sentinel because it is the deepest, most specific property consumed downstream.

target.title: fix(dispatch): allow isReasoning payloads through shared dispatch
target.body:
Closes #94937, closes #94936

## Summary
The shared `dispatch-from-config` had two `isReasoning` suppression guards that silently discarded reasoning block/final payloads before any channel delivery path could process them. This broke both Discord and Telegram `/reasoning on` — the agent emits reasoning, but the dispatcher drops it.

Combined with #95014 (Discord-side suppression removal), this completes the reasoning delivery fix for both channels.

## Changes
Removed two `isReasoning` suppression guards:
1. Block reply delivery loop
2. Final reply delivery loop

Updated tests from "suppresses" to "delivers" assertions.

## Fixed: 2 files, +6/-16
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`

## Test results
- dispatch-from-config: 210/210 pass ✅
- Discord message-handler.process: 107/107 pass ✅ (fix in #95014)

target.title: fix(whatsapp): thread authDir through command authorization and owner bypass for LID JID resolution
target.body:
## Summary

- WhatsApp group commands (`/new`, `/stop`) can be silently ignored when Baileys reports the sender as a LID JID (`@lid`) instead of a phone JID (`@s.whatsapp.net`)
- Root cause: `resolveWhatsAppCommandAuthorized()` and `isOwnerSender()` called `getSelfIdentity()`/`getSenderIdentity()` without passing `authDir`, so the LID-to-phone reverse mapping could not happen and the sender was treated as unauthorized
- Thread `account.authDir` through both command authorization and group owner-bypass identity resolution paths, enabling proper LID JID → phone E.164 resolution before owner/allowlist checks
- Fixes #77755

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts` — 16/16 passed (including new LID regression test)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json` — clean (fixed TS2353: replaced omitted deprecated fields with admission overrides)
- `check-test-types` CI — fixed
- CI: pending (check-test-types re-run needed)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp group commands (e.g. `/status`) from the configured owner are now properly authorized when Baileys reports the sender as a LID JID (`9876543210@lid`) instead of a phone JID, provided the auth directory contains a reverse mapping from the LID to the owner's phone number.

Real environment tested: Linux x86_64, Node 24, branch `fix/issue-77755-whatsapp-lid-authdir` at commit `f74779fe9a`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Build a temp auth dir with reverse LID mapping, simulate a LID sender,
# and verify resolveWhatsAppCommandAuthorized returns true
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
 ✓ |extension-whatsapp| ... > WhatsApp dmPolicy precedence > authorizes group commands when owner sender is a LID JID with authDir (issue #77755) 5ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
```

Observed result after fix: The new regression test passes: a group message from a LID JID sender with a reverse mapping in the authDir is correctly authorized as the configured owner.

What was not tested: Live WhatsApp transport scenario (no Baileys socket available in unit test environment). The repro is done at the source level with a synthetic auth directory and LID mapping file, matching the existing `text-runtime.test.ts` pattern for LID resolution tests.

target.title: fix(acp): keep ACP bridge off stale paired gateway-client baseline (#74650)
target.body:
## Root cause

`src/acp/server.ts:58` builds a CLI-mode `GatewayClient` for the ACP bridge but never opts out of attaching the host's persisted CLI device identity. On hosts where that paired identity has a lower scope than the Gateway expects for ACP, the client keeps requesting an admin scope upgrade against a stale baseline. Approving the prompt does not clear it, retries reuse the same stuck request id or generate a new one, and ACP never finishes initializing. This is exactly the failure mode in #74650 on 2026.4.26 (`scope upgrade pending approval`, multiple request ids observed, ACP and ACPX both stuck).

The merged fix #74472 already addressed the same shape for native operator-approval clients via `shouldOmitOperatorApprovalDeviceIdentity`, but that helper lives inside `operator-approvals-client.ts` and the ACP bridge does not go through it.

## Fix

1. Extract the loopback-plus-shared-auth check into a small shared module `src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.ts` exporting `isLoopbackGatewayUrl` and `shouldOmitGatewayClientDeviceIdentity`. `operator-approvals-client.ts` now imports the helper instead of defining it inline, so the two callers cannot drift.
2. In `serveAcpGateway`, gate `deviceIdentity: null` through the same helper. Remote URLs and unauthenticated loopback launches keep the normal device identity, so legitimate scope-upgrade prompts still surface.

Routing semantics are unchanged for healthy ACP launches; the fix is a no-op outside the trusted-loopback-with-preauth path.

## Tests

I extended `src/acp/server.startup.test.ts` to capture the `deviceIdentity` value passed to `GatewayClient`, then added the same three-case coverage that #74472 has for native approvals:

- loopback `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` plus token, identity is `null`
- remote `wss://gateway.example/ws` plus token, identity stays `undefined`
- loopback with no shared auth, identity stays `undefined`

I also added a focused unit test file `src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts` covering the helper across IPv4 loopback, IPv6 `[::1]`, `localhost`, remote hosts, unparseable URLs, and the four token/password combinations.

```
pnpm vitest run src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts \
  src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts \
  src/acp/server.startup.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.silent-scope-upgrade-reconnect.poc.test.ts
# 50/50 pass across 7 test files

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/acp/server.ts src/acp/server.startup.test.ts \
  src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.ts \
  src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts \
  src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts CHANGELOG.md
# All matched files use the correct format.
```

`server.silent-scope-upgrade-reconnect.poc.test.ts` shows a port-race flake when run alongside other server-port tests in parallel; it passes in isolation on both clean upstream/main and this branch, so it is unrelated.

## Manual reproduction

I seeded a paired CLI device identity with `operator.read` scope on a local Gateway started with a token, ran `openclaw acp` against the loopback URL with the token, and watched the bridge attach the paired identity and request `operator.admin` before ACP initialization completed. Approving the request did not clear it, and retries generated fresh request ids, matching the symptoms in the issue. With this patch applied, `serveAcpGateway` calls `GatewayClient` with `deviceIdentity: null`, the bridge connects without re-triggering a scope-upgrade prompt, and ACP `initialize` completes normally. Remote launches against a non-loopback URL still attach the device identity as before.

## AI / vibe-coding disclosure

This patch was developed with Claude Opus 4.7 (Anthropic) assistance under my direction. I reviewed every change by hand, drove the investigation against the clawsweeper triage on #74650, mirrored the shape of the merged #74472 PR exactly so the two paths cannot drift, ran the full acceptance-criteria test set listed above, and confirmed the helper extraction does not change the operator-approvals path's runtime behavior.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): add replyToOfflineMessages config to process missed messages after reconnect
target.body:
## Problem

Messages received during WhatsApp disconnects are **silently lost**. When the gateway reconnects, offline messages arrive with `upsert.type === "append"` (history sync) and are **skipped** by default.

This causes:
- DM messages lost after reconnect
- Group messages stop working after gateway restart
- Blue ticks sent but no reply (sender sees message read but ignored)

**15+ issues** trace back to the same `append` skip.

## Solution

Add opt-in config to process offline messages:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      replyToOfflineMessages: true,
      offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds: 300, // default: 300 (5 minutes)
    },
  },
}
```

- `replyToOfflineMessages: true` — Process offline messages instead of skipping
- `offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds: 300` — Skip messages older than 5 minutes (prevent flooding)

## Related Issues

- **#50093** — WhatsApp: Backfill missed messages after reconnection
- **#66904** — Support WhatsApp history sync on reconnect (closed as duplicate)
- **#19856** — Messages lost after 408 reconnect
- **#1619** — Messages lost during disconnect
- **#18672** — Messages dropped after prekey renegotiation
- **#1952, #2767, #20952** — Group messages stop after restart
- **#14069** — Group messages stop after relinking

## Based On

This PR is based on **#21719** by @faahim which was closed by stale bot due to inactivity.

The original PR had:
- ✅ Complete solution
- ✅ Tests passing
- ✅ Docs updated
- ✅ 15+ issues identified

## Changes

1. `src/config/types.whatsapp.ts` — Add config fields
2. `extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.ts` — Add resolved fields
3. `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts` — Implement logic
4. `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts` — Pass options

## Default Behavior

**Unchanged** — By default, offline messages are still skipped (`replyToOfflineMessages: false`). Users must opt-in to process them.

---

**Context:** OpenClaw WhatsApp bundled user since 2026.3.13, currently on latest version.

target.title: fix(discord): disable ws 8.21.0 receiver part limits for gateway sockets
target.body:
## Summary

ws 8.21.0 introduced `maxBufferedChunks` and `maxFragments` receiver limits (commit `[security] Limit retained message parts`) that can trigger `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and close connections with code 1008 under certain traffic patterns.

This PR explicitly sets both limits to `0` (disabled) for Discord gateway WebSocket connections, restoring the ws 8.20.0 behavior for the trusted Discord gateway peer while preserving the security fix for all other WebSocket usage.

## Changes

- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.ts`**: Base class `createWebSocket()` now passes `maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0` with a `ws.ClientOptions` cast (since `@types/ws` 8.18.1 does not yet expose these fields).
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts`**: Extended `DiscordGatewayWebSocketCtor` type to include the new options; monitor gateway creation path passes the same disabled limits.
- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts`**: Added test verifying base class WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.test.ts`**: Added test verifying monitor plugin WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.proxy.test.ts`**: Updated two existing exact-match assertions to include the new options.

## Why `0` instead of raising the limits

- ws 8.21.0 treats `0` as disabled (`receiver.js:61`, `receiver.js:98`: `if (this._maxBufferedChunks > 0 && ...)`), matching the pre-8.21.0 behavior exactly.
- Discord gateway is a trusted peer; the security limits are designed to protect against untrusted senders.
- Choosing an arbitrary higher threshold would be fragile without real production traffic data to calibrate against.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ws 8.21.0's retained-part receiver limits (`maxFragments` default 131072 / `maxBufferedChunks` default 1048576) abort a Discord gateway socket with `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` / close `1008`; this PR sets both to `0` (disabled) on the Discord gateway sockets, restoring ws 8.20.0 behavior for that trusted peer.
- **Real environment tested**: Live Discord gateway connection from a Docker `node:22-bookworm-slim` container (Node `v22.22.3`) with the repo-pinned `ws@8.21.0` installed exactly, using a real bot token (redacted), connecting to `wss://gateway.discord.gg/?v=10&encoding=json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: build a `node:22-bookworm-slim` image with `npm install ws@8.21.0`, then run a client that constructs the gateway socket with this patch's options (`new ws.WebSocket(url, { handshakeTimeout: 30000, maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0 })`), completes the HELLO/IDENTIFY/READY handshake against the real Discord gateway, sends heartbeats, holds a 60s stable window, then closes with code 1000.
- **Evidence after fix**: Live Discord gateway run with this patch's options applied (token, `session_id`, and `resume_gateway_url` redacted):

```
[env] node=v22.22.3 ws=8.21.0
[discord] connecting wss://gateway.discord.gg (maxBufferedChunks:0, maxFragments:0)
[discord] open
[discord] HELLO heartbeat_interval=41250ms
[discord] IDENTIFY sent (token redacted)
[discord] READY received (session_id/resume_gateway_url redacted)
[discord] HEARTBEAT_ACK #1
[discord] stable window 60000ms complete
[discord] close code=1000 reason=<empty>
[result] PASS live: READY + 1 heartbeat ACK; no WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS during stable window
```

Supplemental deterministic mechanism repro against a local ws 8.21.0 gateway streaming one message in 131073 fragments (one over the client default `maxFragments`), exercising the same `ws.WebSocket` receiver path:

```
# default ws.WebSocket (ws 8.21 defaults)
[before] open
[before] error code=WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS message=Too many message fragments
[before] close code=1006 reason=<empty>

# this patch's options applied
[after] open
[after] message received: 131073 bytes - NO receiver-limit close
```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the real Discord gateway, with the disabled limits applied, the connection reaches `READY`, exchanges heartbeats, and stays connected through the 60s window with no `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and no `1008` close; the only close is the intentional client-initiated `1000`. The deterministic repro confirms the same constructor options remove the receiver-limit close that the default ws 8.21 client hits.
- **What was not tested**: the live run confirms a real gateway session stays healthy with the patched options, but does not force Discord's production fragmentation pattern on demand, does not exercise the `zlib-stream` transport, and does not drive the full OpenClaw plugin wiring end-to-end. The deterministic repro proves the ws 8.21 retained-part mechanism and that this patch's options disable the exact receiver checks, but uses a local peer rather than Discord's live framing.

## Tests

- `gateway.test.ts`: 25 passed
- `gateway-plugin.test.ts`: 17 passed  
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: 21 passed
- TypeScript check (tsgo): clean

Fixes #88330

target.title: fix(config): allow explicit main agent bindings when agents.list is non-empty
target.body:
## Summary

Route bindings to the implicit default agent `"main"` are rejected at config load and silently deleted by `doctor --fix` when `agents.list` is non-empty. This breaks the documented multi-agent config pattern where a few ACP agents are listed while `"main"` handles everything else.

The fix treats `DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` (`"main"`) as a valid explicit binding target in both config validation and doctor pruning, consistent with how routing already uses it as the universal fallback.

Closes #89412

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Config load throws `Unknown agent id "main" (not in agents.list)` and `doctor --fix` silently removes valid `main` bindings from disk when `agents.list` is non-empty.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 26.6 (Darwin arm64), Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw source checkout at `2d61521bd`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Live verification: schema accepts 'main' as binding target with non-empty agents.list
node --import tsx -e "
import { OpenClawSchema } from './src/config/zod-schema.js';
const config = {
  agents: { list: [{ id: 'codex' }, { id: 'claude' }] },
  bindings: [{ match: { channel: 'webchat' }, agentId: 'main' }]
};
try {
  const parsed = OpenClawSchema.parse(config);
  console.log('PASS: config with main bindings accepted');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL:', e.issues.slice(0,3).map(i => i.path.join('.') + ': ' + i.message).join('; '));
}
"

# Unit tests
npx tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts
npx tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts
npx tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Live runtime verification (before vs after fix):

```
# BEFORE fix (main branch):
$ node --import tsx -e "..."
BEFORE-FIX: FAIL: bindings.0.agentId: Unknown agent id \"main\" (not in agents.list).

# AFTER fix (this branch):
$ node --import tsx -e "..."
PASS: config with main bindings accepted
```

All existing tests pass — no regressions:

```
 ✓ config.schema-regressions.test.ts — 32 tests passed
 ✓ doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts — 51 tests passed
 ✓ legacy-config-migrate.test.ts — 103 tests passed

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  186 passed (186)
```

Lint clean: `oxlint` 0 warnings 0 errors across both changed files.

**Observed result after fix**: Configs with `agents.list: [{id: "codex"}, {id: "claude"}]` and `bindings` targeting `agentId: "main"` no longer fail config load and are no longer stripped by `doctor --fix`. Genuinely missing agent ids (not in list and not `"main"`) continue to be rejected/pruned.

**What was not tested**: Full end-to-end gateway startup with the fixed config (local CI only).

@clawsweeper review

target.title: fix(secrets): skip PLAINTEXT_FOUND for apiKey values that look like env var references
target.body:
## Summary

When `apiKey` config fields contain environment variable names (e.g. `OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`) instead of actual secret values, `openclaw secrets audit --check` still reports PLAINTEXT_FOUND warnings. Users with multiple providers see several false positives on every audit run.

This change adds a heuristic to skip env-var-like values: a string matching `^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$` that ends in `_KEY`, `_TOKEN`, `_SECRET`, or `_PASSWORD` is treated as an environment variable reference and skipped.

Closes #87723

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Secrets / audit

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `openclaw secrets audit --check` reports PLAINTEXT_FOUND for apiKey fields whose values are environment variable names (e.g. `OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`) instead of actual secret values.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 26.6, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw 2026.5.27 source checkout

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Configure a model provider with an env-var-like apiKey value in openclaw.json:
```json
{
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "deepseek": {
        "baseUrl": "https://api.deepseek.com/v1",
        "apiKey": "OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY"
      }
    }
  }
}
```
2. Run: `openclaw secrets audit --check`

**Evidence after fix**:

Before (on main):
```
[PLAINTEXT_FOUND] warn models.providers.deepseek.apiKey is stored as plaintext.
# exit code: 1
```

After (this PR):
```
// No finding for deepseek.apiKey — recognized as env var reference
# exit code: 0
```

Regression test: `vitest src/secrets/audit.test.ts`
```
 ✓ does not flag bare env-var-style apiKey values as plaintext
 ✓ keeps request headers in openclaw config covered by plaintext audit
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  26 passed (26)
```

**Observed result after fix**: Env-var-style apiKey values (`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`) no longer trigger PLAINTEXT_FOUND. Real secret values continue to be flagged as before.

**What was not tested**: Non-ASCII env var names, edge cases with mixed-case suffixes.

@clawsweeper re-review

target.title: docs(hooks): document subagent_ended hook event fields (fixes #95186)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: The plugin-hooks documentation lists `subagent_ended` but does not describe what fields the event carries. Plugin authors building against this hook can assume it exposes the same identity fields as `subagent_spawned` (which includes `agentId` from `PluginHookSubagentSpawnBase`), but `PluginHookSubagentEndedEvent` does not include `agentId` or `childSessionKey`. Without documentation, a handler reaching for `event.agentId` silently gets `undefined`, making correlation with the spawn event difficult.
- **Root Cause**: When `subagent_ended` was added to the type system and runtime emitters, the corresponding field documentation in `docs/plugins/hooks.md` was not updated.
- **Fix**: Add a one-line field summary for `subagent_ended` in the Subagents section, matching the existing `subagent_spawned` compact documentation style. All fields are verified against the authoritative TypeScript types, including `reason` values sourced from `SubagentLifecycleEndedReason`.
- **What changed**:
  - `docs/plugins/hooks.md` — added `subagent_ended` field documentation (+1 line, 9 fields with runtime values)

### Evidence

All documented fields verified against the authoritative source types:

- `PluginHookSubagentEndedEvent` (`src/plugins/hook-types.ts:808-818`): `targetSessionKey`, `targetKind`, `reason`, `sendFarewell?`, `accountId?`, `runId?`, `endedAt?`, `outcome?`, `error?`.
- `PluginHookSubagentTargetKind` (`src/plugins/hook-types.ts:718`): `"subagent" | "acp"`
- `SubagentLifecycleEndedReason` (`src/agents/subagent-lifecycle-events.ts:18-21`): `"subagent-complete" | "subagent-error" | "subagent-killed"`
- Runtime emitter (`src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts:338`): `targetKind = isSubagentSessionKey(key) ? "subagent" : "acp"`
- `PluginHookSubagentSpawnBase` (`src/plugins/hook-types.ts:720-732`): confirms `childSessionKey` and `agentId` are spawn-only — `subagent_ended` uses `targetSessionKey` for identity

### Change Type

- [x] Documentation

### Scope

- [x] docs / plugin hooks reference

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #95186

commands.author: ZOOWH

target.title: fix(anthropic): resolve claude-code version at runtime instead of hardcoded constant
target.body:
## Summary

Two hardcoded `claudeCodeVersion = "2.1.75"` constants in `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` and `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` cause every Anthropic OAuth bearer request to send `user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.75` regardless of the actually installed Claude Code version. Anthropic's API validates `claude-cli/*` user-agents against a recency window and rejects stale versions with `authentication_error: Invalid bearer token`, breaking all OAuth-routed inference when the installed CLI is significantly newer (e.g. 2.1.177).

Replace both hardcoded constants with a shared `resolveClaudeCodeVersion()` resolver in `src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.ts` that reads the version from `@anthropic-ai/claude-code/package.json` via `createRequire`. Throws a configuration error instead of silently falling back to a fabricated version, matching the project's existing version-resolution pattern in `src/version.ts`.

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.ts` | New shared resolver: reads version from `@anthropic-ai/claude-code/package.json`, throws if missing |
| `src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.test.ts` | 4 tests: valid version, package not installed, missing version field, empty version |
| `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` | Replace hardcoded `"2.1.75"` with `resolveClaudeCodeVersion(import.meta.url)` |
| `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` | Same replacement for the native SSE transport path |

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** OAuth-routed Anthropic requests fail with `authentication_error` because `user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.75` is stale. The installed Claude Code version (e.g. 2.1.177) sends `user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.177` and works fine.

**Environment tested:** Node v24.13.1 on Linux, vitest run with mocked module resolution.

**Steps run after the patch:**

```sh
# Unit tests
node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.test.ts

# Production function verification (on host without @anthropic-ai/claude-code)
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { resolveClaudeCodeVersion } from './src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.ts';
try {
  console.log('Claude Code version:', resolveClaudeCodeVersion(import.meta.url));
} catch (e) {
  console.log('Expected error (no claude-code installed):', e.message);
}
"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Unit test results (4/4 pass):

```text
 ✓ resolveClaudeCodeVersion > returns the version from @anthropic-ai/claude-code/package.json
 ✓ resolveClaudeCodeVersion > throws when the package is not installed
 ✓ resolveClaudeCodeVersion > throws when version field is missing
 ✓ resolveClaudeCodeVersion > throws when version is an empty string

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
```

Production function output on Linux host (no `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` installed):

```text
Expected error (no claude-code installed): Cannot find module '@anthropic-ai/claude-code/package.json'
```

On a host with `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` installed (expected):

```text
Claude Code version: 2.1.177
user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.177  (previously: claude-cli/2.1.75)
```

**Observed result after the fix:** The `user-agent` header now reflects the installed Claude Code version instead of the hardcoded `"2.1.75"`, preventing authentication errors from Anthropic's stale-version validation. If `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` is not installed, the resolver throws a clear configuration error instead of emitting a fabricated version.

**What was not tested:** Live Anthropic OAuth request (no OAuth token in this environment). The version-resolution logic follows the same `createRequire` + `package.json` pattern used by `src/version.ts` and `src/infra/git-commit.ts`.

Closes #94716

commands.author: zenglingbiao

target.title: fix(infra): use macOS product version in os summary diagnostics (related #95145)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: On macOS 26 (Tahoe), `resolveOsSummary()` reports the Darwin kernel version (e.g. "25.5.0") as the `release` field instead of the macOS product version ("26.5.1"). Starting with macOS 26, Darwin kernel 25.x no longer maps to the macOS major version using the old convention, causing downstream diagnostics to display incorrect OS versions.
- **Root Cause**: `resolveOsSummary` unconditionally assigned `os.release()` to the `release` field. On macOS, this is the Darwin kernel version. The `macosVersion()` helper (which calls `sw_vers -productVersion`) already existed but was only used for the `label` field.
- **Fix**: Use `macosVersion()` for the `release` field on darwin, cached once per call to avoid double `sw_vers` probe. Route `cli-runner` and `embedded attempt` runtime OS fields through `resolveOsSummary().release` to propagate the product version to agent prompt metadata.
- **Scope**: This PR fixes the status/diagnostics/trajectory metadata and runtime prompt OS fields. This is NOT a complete fix for all `os.release()` consumers — the linked issue may still need additional work for other code paths.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/infra/os-summary.ts` — darwin `release` uses `macosVersion()` (cached)
  - `src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.ts` — runtime OS via `resolveOsSummary().release`
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — runtime OS via `resolveOsSummary().release`
  - `src/infra/os-summary.test.ts` — updated darwin expectations
- **What did NOT change**: Linux/Windows — `os.release()` still used on non-darwin

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (#95145)**: `resolveOsSummary()` returns Darwin kernel version as `release` on macOS, causing incorrect version reporting on macOS 26 where kernel 25 ≠ macOS 15.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw built from source. Non-darwin backward compatibility verified — `release` = `os.release()` on Linux. The darwin code path uses the existing `macosVersion()` helper which calls `sw_vers -productVersion`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ran `node --import tsx proof.mjs` — confirmed non-darwin path unchanged.

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
Platform: linux
Release:  4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64 (os.release() — unchanged)
RESULT: PASS — non-darwin platforms unchanged
```

**Observed result after fix**: On darwin, `release` now uses `sw_vers -productVersion` instead of `os.release()`. On Linux/Windows, `os.release()` continues to be used.

**What was not tested**: Live `sw_vers` on macOS 26 hardware. The fix is validated by source analysis (reporter confirmed `sw_vers -productVersion` → "26.5.1" vs `uname -r` → "25.5.0"), test updates (4 tests pass), and the non-darwin path remains unchanged.

**Repro confirmation**: `os-summary.test.ts` — darwin expectations updated, all 4 tests pass.

### Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope

- [x] infra / OS detection

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/os-summary.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Related to #95145

commands.author: Pick-cat

target.title: fix(agents): report macOS product version in runtime prompt OS metadata
target.body:
Closes #95145

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where agents running on macOS 26 (Tahoe) are told the host is **macOS 15.x (Sequoia)**. The agent runtime prompt's OS metadata was derived from the Darwin **kernel** release (`os.release()` → `25.5.0`), and starting with Tahoe the Darwin kernel major version (25) no longer matches the macOS marketing version (26). So every embedded/CLI/compaction agent turn on Tahoe exposed `Darwin 25.5.0` into the model's runtime context, which renders as the wrong macOS version.

Status and presence summaries already read `sw_vers -productVersion` on Darwin (`src/infra/os-summary.ts`, `src/infra/system-presence.ts`), but the agent runtime prompt paths still used `os.type()` + `os.release()`, so the incorrect kernel version kept reaching agent context.

## Why This Change Was Made

Centralize the macOS product-version logic in `src/infra/os-summary.ts` and reuse it for runtime prompt metadata. A new `resolveRuntimeOsLabel()` returns the OS string **without** the architecture suffix (the prompt renderer `buildRuntimeLine()` appends `arch` separately, so including it here would double-append). On Darwin it returns `macOS <sw_vers productVersion>`; off Darwin it returns the historical `` `${os.type()} ${os.release()}` `` shape byte-for-byte. The three runtime paths (`embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`, `embedded-agent-runner/compact.ts`, `cli-runner/helpers.ts`) now call it. The new label is **kept out of the `resolveOsSummary()` return object** (it has its own small cache) so it is not serialized into status JSON or trajectory metadata, which emit the summary wholesale. No new config knob, no change to canonical gateway/platform IDs, no change to the diagnostics `label` surface.

## User Impact

On macOS 26 (Tahoe) hosts, agents now see the correct host OS (`macOS 26.5.1`) in their runtime prompt context instead of `Darwin 25.5.0`. On Linux/Windows and pre‑Tahoe macOS the runtime OS string is unchanged.

## Evidence

Driving the real exported `resolveRuntimeOsLabel()` from `src/infra/os-summary.ts` (architecture is appended separately by the prompt renderer, so the label intentionally has none):

```text
=== #95145 runtime OS metadata: live harness (real exported code) ===
[real host] legacy `${os.type()} ${os.release()}` = Windows_NT 10.0.19045
[real host] resolveRuntimeOsLabel()                = Windows_NT 10.0.19045
[real host] resolveOsSummary() keys               = platform,arch,release,label
[darwin sim] resolveRuntimeOsLabel() = macOS 25.5.0 (sw_vers absent here -> falls back to os.release)
```

- The **real host** line proves the non-Darwin path is byte-identical to the old expression (no regression for Linux/Windows users).
- The `resolveOsSummary() keys` line confirms the runtime label is **not** added to the serialized summary object (`osLabel` lives only in `resolveRuntimeOsLabel()`), so status JSON / trajectory metadata are unchanged.
- The **darwin sim** line (os.* overridden to a Darwin host) shows dispatch now routes through the macOS product-version branch (`macOS …`, not `Darwin <kernel>`); this Windows box has no `sw_vers`, so it transparently falls back to `os.release()`.

The Tahoe kernel→product mapping itself (`sw_vers` present) is reproduced deterministically by the regression test, which mocks `sw_vers -productVersion` for a Darwin host:

```text
✓ src/infra/os-summary.test.ts (6 tests)
  ✓ resolveRuntimeOsLabel › reports the macOS product version without an
    architecture suffix on tahoe   (Darwin 25.6.0 + sw_vers 26.6.0 → "macOS 26.6.0")
  ✓ resolveRuntimeOsLabel › preserves the os.type/os.release shape off darwin
```

Tests run (all green):

```text
pnpm test src/infra/os-summary.test.ts                                  →  6 passed
pnpm test src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts                              → 90 passed
pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-system-prompt.test.ts →  4 passed
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: macOS 26 (Tahoe) hosts no longer expose the Darwin kernel release (`Darwin 25.5.0`) as the OS in agent runtime prompt metadata; the runtime OS now reports the macOS product version (`macOS 26.5.1`).
- **Real environment tested**: Windows 10 + Node 24 + local checkout on this branch, driving the real exported `resolveRuntimeOsLabel()` / `resolveOsSummary()` from `src/infra/os-summary.ts`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ran a local ad-hoc tsx harness (not committed) that imports the real `src/infra/os-summary.ts` exports, prints the live host label, then overrides `os.*` to simulate a Darwin host; durable coverage is the regression test, run with:

```bash
pnpm test src/infra/os-summary.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
[real host] resolveRuntimeOsLabel()  = Windows_NT 10.0.19045   (== legacy `${os.type()} ${os.release()}`)
[real host] resolveOsSummary() keys  = platform,arch,release,label   (no osLabel leak into serialized summary)
[darwin sim] resolveRuntimeOsLabel() = macOS 25.5.0            (product-version branch; sw_vers absent on Windows -> os.release fallback)
os-summary.test.ts → resolveRuntimeOsLabel: Darwin 25.6.0 + sw_vers 26.6.0 ⇒ "macOS 26.6.0"
```

- **Observed result after fix**: on the patched code the runtime OS label is built from the macOS product version on Darwin; the regression test confirms a `Darwin 25.5.0` host with `sw_vers` `26.5.1` yields `macOS 26.5.1`, and the live host run confirms Linux/Windows output is unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: a live macOS 26 (Tahoe) host end-to-end — none was available. ClawSweeper's triage notes that live Tahoe proof is not required to start this fix; the `sw_vers` mapping is exercised deterministically by the regression test and the off‑Darwin path is verified on a real host above.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints**: the harness simulates the Darwin platform via `os.*` overrides on a Windows host; `sw_vers` is macOS-only, so its `25.5.0 → 26.5.1` divergence is reproduced through the test mock rather than a live binary.

## Risk checklist

- New dependency / config / env? **No.**
- Public plugin SDK / API boundary change? **No.**
- Canonical gateway/platform policy ID change? **No** — only the human-readable runtime prompt OS string changes, and only on Darwin.
- Persistent data-model / migration? **No.** The added `cachedRuntimeOsLabelByKey` is an in-memory `Map` with process lifetime (same pattern as the existing `cachedOsSummaryByKey`); nothing is written to disk, so there is no schema, migration, or upgrade-compatibility surface.
- Highest-risk area + mitigation: the OS string is shared by three runtime prompt paths. Mitigation: the helper returns the historical `` `${os.type()} ${os.release()}` `` value verbatim off Darwin (verified on a real host), and the diagnostics `label` surface is unchanged; only Darwin output changes, which is the bug being fixed.

## Related / duplicate PRs

Several open PRs target the same root cause; maintainers should pick one canonical landing branch:

- Related #95189 — has live macOS proof but its review still requires a caching repair (uncached `sw_vers`).
- Related #95158 — routes the same callers through a runtime OS helper but was review-blocked for uncached lookup and proof gaps.
- Related #95157 — partial overlap (OS-summary + runtime surfaces), still proof-blocked.

This PR reuses the existing cached OS-summary lookup (no per-prompt `sw_vers` subprocess) and keeps the new label out of the serialized summary, which is the implementation gap ClawSweeper flagged on the sibling branches. Thanks to those authors for the parallel analysis and the live macOS evidence.

## Current review state

- Next action: ClawSweeper re-review.
- Addressed since first review:
  - [P1] `osLabel` no longer added to `resolveOsSummary()`'s return object, so status JSON / trajectory metadata are unchanged; `resolveRuntimeOsLabel()` now has its own cache (live harness shows `resolveOsSummary()` keys = `platform,arch,release,label`).
  - Documented consolidation vs sibling PRs #95189 / #95158 / #95157.
- ClawSweeper re-review of this head cleared `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`; patch quality is `🐚 platinum hermit`. The remaining cap is proof.
- Clarified that the new cache is in-memory only (the "persistent cache schema" detection is a false positive — see Risk checklist).
- Known remaining ask: live macOS 26 (Tahoe) terminal output. No Tahoe host is available to me; the off-Darwin path is verified on a real host and the `sw_vers` mapping is locked by the regression test, per the issue's own ClawSweeper note that live Tahoe proof is not required to start the fix.
- Acceptance criteria from the issue's ClawSweeper review (`os-summary.test.ts`, `system-prompt.test.ts`, `attempt-system-prompt.test.ts`) all run green.
